pages.full_text:
[Full road, looking from Craignure, where we camped.] [Duart Castle] We walked the last 2½ miles because the railway ended after only one mile at Torosay Castle

2½ miles - a lovely walk, around the bay, to Duart Castle. 7-8 foot-thick walls. Home of MacCleans. I met Lochlan in the Courtyard & his wife, Mary, & they remembered the Banks. Spanish prisoner in the dungeon. Homemade cakes. The engine driver, & his wife, who also sold tickets
photo_captions: Full road, looking from Craignure, where we camped. [Duart Castle] We walked the last 2½ miles because the railway ended after only one mile at Torosay Castle
pages.notes: Handwritten page number in top-left corner is unclear — characters present but not legible as a number. Photo caption runs diagonally alongside the glued-in photograph. 'MacCleans' likely refers to Clan MacLean, the historic owners of Duart Castle on the Isle of Mull. No year stated on this page; volume A covers 1989–2003.

pages.full_text:
at the castle, had a border terrier too. We got back at 6. A super afternoon. Peter walked back across the bay, getting his feet wet his new sandals baked blue. It's lucky the tide was out. Tonic has ticks again.

   We are going out for a pub supper.

[route sketch: Duart Bay / Duart Castle]

Cotton
We met Rita & Larry from Mississippi. He was a bluegrass singer, who now works in art in Aberdeen, but they have sold up & are moving home with pictures to sell.
   The food was OK... in parts.

__Thu 5th Aug__

   We set off for Iona, which was rather a disappointment. Lots of people milling around like cattle.
   But in the cloisters, a voice said "Well, hello." + it was Caren Vignos, convalescing after Bekka's wedding in June. She is in a retreat house for 5 nights. So we had a very good time with her over lunch.
pages.route_sketch_description: Simple hand-drawn sketch showing a bay with a castle silhouette. Labelled 'Duart Bay' and 'Duart Castle' on the left, with a coastline and what appears to be trees on the right. No arrows or route direction indicated.
pages.notes: No year visible on this page. 'Thu 5th Aug' — within volume A (1989–2003), 5 August fell on a Thursday in 1993 and 1999 among others; cannot determine year from content alone. 'Cotton' above the meeting with Rita & Larry appears to be the name of the pub where they had supper. 'Tonic' appears to be the family dog. 'Caren Vignos' — surname could also read [?Vignos]; 'Bekka' could be short for Rebecca. 'Iona' is underlined in the original.
